back_translation: Later, Solomon formed an alliance with Pharaoh king of Egypt by marrying Pharaoh's daughter. He brought her to the City of David until he had finished building his palace, the house of the LORD, and the wall around Jerusalem.
The people, however, were still sacrificing on the high places, because a house for the Name of the LORD had not yet been built up to that time.
Solomon loved the LORD, walking in the statutes of David his father, except that he still sacrificed and burned incense on the high places.
The king went to Gibeon to sacrifice there, for it was the greatest high place. Solomon offered a thousand burnt offerings on that altar.
At Gibeon the LORD appeared to Solomon in a dream at night, and God said, “Ask what you want me to give you.”

Solomon said, “You have shown great steadfast love to your servant David my father, because he walked before you in faithfulness, righteousness, and uprightness of heart toward you; and you have kept this great steadfast love for him by giving him a son to sit on his throne this day.
And now, O LORD my God, you have made your servant king in place of David my father, but I am only a little child; I do not know how to go out and come in to lead the people.
Your servant is among the people you have chosen, a great nation that cannot be counted or numbered because they are so many.
Therefore give your servant a discerning heart to govern your people and to judge between good and evil; for who is able to govern this great people of yours?”
This request was pleasing in the eyes of the Lord, because Solomon had asked for this.
So God said to him, “Because you asked for this and did not ask for long life or riches for yourself, or the life of your enemies, but asked for understanding to discern justice,
behold, I will do according to your word. Behold, I give you a heart full of wisdom and understanding, so that there has never been anyone like you before, nor will anyone like you arise again.
Moreover, I give you what you did not ask—both riches and honor—so that all your days there will be no king like you.
And if you walk in my ways, keeping my statutes and commandments as David your father walked, I will lengthen your days.”

Then Solomon awoke, and behold, it was a dream. He came to Jerusalem, stood before the ark of the covenant of the Lord, offered burnt offerings and peace offerings, and made a feast for all his servants.
At that time two prostitutes came to the king and stood before him.
One woman said, “Please, my lord, this woman and I live in the same house, and I gave birth while she was in the house.
On the third day after I gave birth, this woman also gave birth. We were together; there was no stranger with us in the house, only the two of us.
During the night this woman's son died, because she lay on him.
So she rose at midnight and took my son from beside me while your maidservant was asleep, and laid him in her bosom, and laid her dead son in my bosom.
When I rose in the morning to nurse my son, behold, he was dead. But when I examined him in the morning, behold, he was not the son I had borne.”
The other woman said, “No, the living one is my son and the dead one is yours.” But the first insisted, “No, the dead one is yours and the living one is mine.” So they argued before the king.
Then the king said, “This one says, ‘My son is alive and your son is dead,’ and that one says, ‘No, your son is dead and my son is alive.’”
The king said further, “Bring me a sword.” So they brought a sword before the king.
And the king said, “Cut the living child in two, and give half to one and half to the other.”

Then the woman who was the mother of the living child spoke to the king, for her heart yearned with compassion for her son, and she said, “Please, my lord, give her the living baby; do not kill him!” But the other said, “He shall be neither mine nor yours; cut him!”
Then the king gave his ruling, “Give the living baby to the first woman; do not kill him; she is his mother.”
When all Israel heard the judgment that the king had given, they stood in awe of him, for they saw that the wisdom of God was in him to administer justice.
